metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Storage design decision (or the process_status processor spec) that `claimed_session` must be updated only when the incoming STATUS event is the FORK WINNER (i.e., apply it in the same branch that updates `state['status']`), and add a regression-test case for the concurrent-claim / fork-resolution scenario.
findings.finding: The fork-resolution path in process_status applies only the WINNING event's status to state — but the plan does not specify that claimed_session must also be gated on the fork winner, risking an implementation that records the losing session's id.
findings.location: Design decisions / Storage section; reducer/_processors.py process_status (lines 128–167)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Storage decision, add: 'In process_status, set claimed_session only in the branch that applies the winning status (both the normal path and the fork-winner sub-branch); never set it when the existing chain wins the fork tie-break.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the complete reducer fold rule for `claimed_session`: the initial value in `make_initial_state`, the exact line in `process_status` where it is written (fork-winner branch vs. normal branch vs. both), and the tie-break policy when the winning STATUS event carries no session id.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `claimed_session` as a new reducer state key but provides no rule for how `process_status` folds `data["session"]` into it under fork resolution — the existing fork tie-break in `process_status` applies `data.get("status")` to the winner but is silent on whether `claimed_session` follows the winner's data or is first-write-wins.
findings.location: Design decisions > Storage section; src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:process_status
findings.suggested_fix: In the Storage design decision, add: 'Set `state["claimed_session"] = data.get("session")` in the normal branch of `process_status`; in the fork branch, apply it only when the incoming event wins (parallel to `state["status"]` assignment). Seed `make_initial_state` with `"claimed_session": None`.'

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Unify terminology: choose either 'SessionStart hook' or 'capture shim' and use it consistently across Design Decisions, Success Criteria, and AC (or add a parenthetical equating them on first use).
findings.finding: The plan uses 'SessionStart hook' (Design Decisions) and 'capture shim' (Success Criteria, AC) to describe the same local-CLI capture mechanism, introducing terminology drift that could cause implementers to treat them as distinct components.
findings.location: Design Decisions: 'Reliable capture' sub-section; Success Criteria item 4; Acceptance Criteria last item
findings.suggested_fix: In the Reliable capture sub-item, write: 'the local CLI needs a capture shim (a SessionStart hook) that exports REBAR_SESSION_ID' — then use 'capture shim' uniformly in SC and AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out procedure (e.g., 'strip `claimed_session` from reducer state via a migration script' or 'the field is ignored-on-read by older versions, so revert is: deploy old reducer and re-compact') to satisfy T4's reversibility requirement.
findings.finding: The plan asserts compaction-safety and older-clone forward compatibility but provides no rollback/back-out plan if the `claimed_session` state key or `data["session"]` payload causes unexpected reducer failures or data corruption in production clones.
findings.location: Design decisions / Storage; Success Criteria 'survives compaction'; AC 'Backward/forward compatible + compaction-safe'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback path' subsection under Storage decisions: since the field is additive and ignored by old reducers, the rollback is 'redeploy old reducer version; `claimed_session` in existing snapshots is silently ignored.' Document this explicitly so operators have a stated recovery procedure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the post-rollback schema state of compacted snapshots: assert that older reducer versions must tolerate unknown state keys (e.g., pass-through unknown keys during compaction) and add a regression test covering a compact-then-rollback cycle so `claimed_session` is either preserved or the data-loss is explicitly accepted and documented.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the feature is 'compaction-safe' and 'tolerated by older clones' on rollback, but never specifies the schema STATE AFTER rollback for compacted snapshots that already contain the `claimed_session` key. If a snapshot is compacted with `claimed_session` present and the feature is then rolled back, an older reducer silently drops the key on the next read — effectively losing the attribution data — or may error if it validates state keys strictly. The plan must explicitly address this post-rollback state.
findings.location: Design decisions / Storage section; AC 'Backward/forward compatible + compaction-safe (regression-tested)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit statement: 'The compactor MUST pass through unknown reducer state keys (including claimed_session) verbatim rather than dropping them, so a rollback does not destroy already-recorded attribution data. Regression test: write a claim with session id, compact, reload with the pre-feature reducer, verify unknown keys are preserved or verify the accepted data-loss boundary.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly mark which AC items are required to close the epic (P0) vs. which are follow-on / nice-to-have (P1), especially for the per-harness shims.
findings.finding: The AC mixes essential deliverables (storage, resolver, compaction safety) with harness shims (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) in a flat unranked list, making it ambiguous whether all shims are required to close the epic or are nice-to-have stretch goals.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (full list) and Success Criteria — '[ ] Session id is attributable across the popular OSS harnesses (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Cursor) through a portable contract.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical or tag: e.g., mark Claude Code shim as '(required — primary harness)', Codex/OpenCode as '(required — env var in resolver)', and Cursor as '(stretch — shim mechanism TBD in child story)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the OpenCode entry: either add an OpenCode shim to the AC shim-delivery item, or add a note explaining that OPENCODE_SESSION_ID is injected natively (no shim needed) so both sections agree on what 'attributable' requires for OpenCode.
findings.finding: OpenCode is listed as a covered harness in Success Criteria ('Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Cursor') but is absent from the AC shim-delivery item ('Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims'), creating a cross-section contradiction about what must actually be shipped.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'Session id is attributable across the popular OSS harnesses (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Cursor)'; Acceptance Criteria: 'Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims'
findings.suggested_fix: If OpenCode injects OPENCODE_SESSION_ID natively, annotate the Success Criteria item: '(OpenCode: native env var; Claude Code local / Codex / Cursor: shims)' and adjust AC accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike/probe step that verifies Claude Code CLI exposes a pre-claim hook (or equivalent mechanism) and that `REBAR_SESSION_ID` is readable by rebar before the `open->in_progress` transition fires.
findings.finding: The SessionStart hook mechanism for the local Claude Code CLI is asserted as 'a capture shim' but the plan includes no spike or probe verifying that Claude Code CLI actually exposes a hook that fires before a claim event, making this an unvalidated assumption about an external system's integration capability.
findings.location: Design decisions — 'Reliable capture' bullet; AC — 'Local-CLI capture shim ships'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a child story: 'Spike: verify Claude Code CLI hook contract' — instrument a minimal hook that prints the session id before a synthetic claim event, confirm timing, and gate the shim story on passing results. If no hook exists, fall back to a wrapper-script pattern and document the decision.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split `decahedral-flaming-agouti` so the FORCE_CLOSE bug fix is its own child (or a separate tracked item), keeping the resolver-unification child focused on a single concern.
findings.finding: Child `decahedral-flaming-agouti` bundles two heterogeneous change kinds in one unit: a feature/refactor (unify session-id resolver) AND what appears to be an unrelated bug fix (FORCE_CLOSE), as signalled by the structural 'and' in its title. A bug fix and a new feature have independent reasons to change, independent reviewers/risk profiles, and independent release value.
findings.location: Decomposition state — child `decahedral-flaming-agouti`: "Unify session-id resolution into one shared, ordered resolver (+ fix FORCE_CLOSE"
findings.suggested_fix: Create a dedicated child ticket for the FORCE_CLOSE fix with its own AC, and remove the parenthetical from `decahedral-flaming-agouti`'s scope. The resolver-unification child should reference the fix only if there is a genuine technical dependency (e.g., the resolver refactor exposed or required the fix), in which case note that dependency explicitly rather than bundling.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and cite the actual env var names exported by OpenCode and Codex (OpenAI) runtimes (link to their source or docs), and note any 'not natively exported — shim required' caveats analogous to the Claude Code local-CLI caveat.
findings.finding: The env var names `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` and `CODEX_THREAD_ID` are asserted as the actual variables exported by the OpenCode and Codex (OpenAI) runtimes respectively, but no source or documentation reference is cited. If either name is wrong, the resolver silently skips it and the AC 'Session id is attributable across the popular OSS harnesses' silently fails for that harness — with no runtime error to surface the mismatch.
findings.location: Design Decisions — Env-var contract; Success Criteria bullet 5; AC bullet 7
findings.suggested_fix: Add a small evidence table in the Design Decisions section, e.g.: | Harness | Env var | Source | Natively exported? | — one row per harness — before the resolver list is finalised.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (with a codebase citation, OSS source link, or spike result) that `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` and `CODEX_THREAD_ID` are the exact env var names exported by those harnesses before committing them to the resolver contract.
findings.finding: OPENCODE_SESSION_ID and CODEX_THREAD_ID are named as integration points in the env-var resolver, but no repo evidence (code, docs, tests, OSS survey notes) confirms these are the actual env var names exported by OpenCode and OpenAI Codex CLI. If either name is wrong, the resolver silently skips that harness with no error.
findings.location: Design decisions — 'Env-var contract (ordered, extensible resolver)': `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` and `CODEX_THREAD_ID`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike result or docs citation confirming the Claude Code CLI hook event name (`SessionStart` or equivalent) and config mechanism before committing the capture-shim implementation story.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the local CLI needs a SessionStart hook that exports REBAR_SESSION_ID' but does not verify that the Claude Code CLI actually exposes a hookable `SessionStart` event type. This is a new, never-before-used external capability in this codebase, and the plan provides no spike, docs reference, or verification artifact.
findings.location: Design decisions — 'Reliable capture': 'the local CLI needs a SessionStart hook that exports REBAR_SESSION_ID'. AC: 'Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID` is an actual env var exported by the Claude Code remote runner (cite docs or a test fixture), or mark it as provisional/TBD in the design.
findings.finding: The plan names `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID` as a secondary field captured into `remote_session`, but no repo evidence confirms this env var is actually present in the Claude Code remote runner environment (distinct from `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`).
findings.location: Design decisions — 'Env-var contract': '`CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID` captured as a secondary `remote_session` field'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'S1 (6014) — shared resolver module' to c557's 'Depends on' list.
findings.finding: Child c557 (S5) extends the shared resolver's var list (_SESSION_ID_VARS in session_id.py) — an artifact exclusively created by child 6014 (S1) — but declares only 'Depends on: S2', omitting S1 as a dependency. If stories are sequenced or parallelized without this constraint, S5 could execute before S1's module exists.
findings.location: child c557-8f46-0c16-4bf5 — 'Depends on' section
findings.suggested_fix: In child c557's 'Depends on' section, add: '- S1 (the shared resolver module and _SESSION_ID_VARS list exist).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether 7656 genuinely requires S5 (harness provenance tag) for its ACs; if not, change its dependency to 'S1 + S2' and update its ACs to reflect what it actually tests.
findings.finding: Child 7656 (S6, Codex/Cursor shims) declares 'Depends on: S5' but its own Acceptance Criteria (exporting REBAR_SESSION_ID and verifying 'a Codex claim records claimed_session') only require S1 (resolver recognizes REBAR_SESSION_ID) and S2 (recording exists). S5 adds CODEX_THREAD_ID to the resolver and harness provenance, neither of which 7656's ACs reference. The S5 dependency unnecessarily blocks 7656's delivery until S5 is complete.
findings.location: child 7656-0a67-e881-4131 — 'Depends on' section and Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: If 7656's shims only export REBAR_SESSION_ID and verify claimed_session (not claim_harness), change 'Depends on: S5' to 'Depends on: S2 (the record path exists)' and optionally add a note that S5 will later enrich claims with a harness tag.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact read surface for `claimed_session` (e.g., 'add `session` field to `rebar show <id>` JSON output and human display') so the AC is evaluable by running a deterministic command.
findings.finding: The AC 'Read surface exposes the claiming session id for a ticket' does not specify what interface or command an executor should implement — CLI field on `rebar show`, a new subcommand, structured JSON output, or something else — making it both ambiguous (E2) and not measurable as written (F1).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — '[ ] Read surface exposes the claiming session id for a ticket.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Read surface exposes the claiming session id for a ticket' with something like: 'Running `rebar show <ticket-id>` (or equivalent) includes a `claimed_session` field in both human-readable and `--json` output when a session was recorded; the field is absent (not null/empty) when none was set.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended shim form for Codex and Cursor (e.g., shell wrapper script, `.env` injection, harness config hook) so the AC is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The AC 'Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims' does not define what form the Codex/Cursor shims take (unlike Claude Code, which has a named SessionStart hook), leaving the executor to choose an implementation mechanism for those two harnesses.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — '[ ] Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a design description for the read surface (e.g., `rebar status <ticket>` output field, JSON key in `rebar show`, or API accessor) so the 'Read surface exposes the claiming session id' AC item has a concrete, verifiable target.
findings.finding: The 'Read surface exposes the claiming session id for a ticket' AC item has no corresponding design description in the plan body — no mechanism (CLI subcommand, JSON field, status output, API) is specified, making this a deliverable with no design backing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Read surface exposes the claiming session id for a ticket'; What / Design Decisions sections
findings.suggested_fix: In the Design Decisions section, add a 'Read surface' sub-item: e.g., 'claimed_session is exposed as a field in the ticket's computed state, readable via rebar show --json and included in rebar status output when non-null.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what 'regression-tested' means for the compaction/backward-compat requirement (e.g., 'an existing fixture-based compaction test is extended to assert claimed_session survives a compact round-trip'), or move test strategy to a child story and remove the parenthetical from the epic AC.
findings.finding: The AC item 'Backward/forward compatible + compaction-safe (regression-tested)' adds a 'regression-tested' qualifier that implies a test deliverable, but no test strategy, test location, or test approach is described anywhere in the plan body, leaving this qualifier ungrounded.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Backward/forward compatible + compaction-safe (regression-tested)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation deliverable to the AC: at minimum, a `docs/session-tracking.md` (or ADR) covering the env-var resolver contract, how to install each harness shim, and the `claimed_session` storage shape — and ensure 'Full design doc lives with the driving session' is replaced with a repo-visible artifact reference.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new user-facing env-var contract (ordered resolver list across 5+ vars) and new per-harness capture shims (Claude Code CLI, Codex, Cursor), but the Acceptance Criteria include no documentation deliverable — no ADR, no user-facing config reference, no shim setup guide.
findings.location: Design decisions / Env-var contract; AC (full list); Success Criteria 'Works in LOCAL Claude Code CLI'; 'Session id is attributable across popular OSS harnesses'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line: '- [ ] `docs/session-tracking.md` (or equivalent ADR) published in-repo, covering: env-var resolver order and rationale, per-harness shim installation, storage shape, and consent/provenance guidance.' Update the 'full design doc' reference to point to this artifact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the extensibility mechanism for the env-var resolver (e.g., ordered list in a config section, a REBAR_SESSION_VARS env override, or a documented constant with a single-edit policy) so 'extensible' is actionable, not aspirational.
findings.finding: The env-var resolver is described as 'ordered, extensible' but the plan does not specify whether the list is configurable (e.g., via a config file or plugin registry) or hardcoded; if hardcoded, adding a new harness requires a code change, undermining the 'extensible' claim.
findings.location: Design decisions — 'Env-var contract' bullet; AC — 'Env-var contract implemented as the ordered, extensible resolver above'
findings.suggested_fix: Define the resolver list as a named, documented constant (e.g., `SESSION_VAR_PRECEDENCE` in a config or constants module) with a comment explaining the ordering rationale, so adding a new harness is a one-line, reviewable change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC item requiring the env-var contract, `claimed_session` reducer key, reconciler exclusion rationale, and consent/provenance sensitivity decision to be captured in a committed ADR or CLAUDE.md update before the epic is closed.
findings.finding: The novel architectural decision (session capture contract, env-var resolver, reducer key `claimed_session`, reconciler exclusion) is documented only in 'the driving session's design doc', which is not a committed artifact that travels with the codebase; no ADR or CLAUDE.md update is required by the plan.
findings.location: Context section — 'Full design doc lives with the driving session'; AC — no ADR/doc update item

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the Design Decisions (or a child story) the specific command/flag and output format that exposes `claimed_session` (e.g. `rebar show <ticket>` prints `claiming_session: <id>`, or a dedicated `rebar session` query), so the read surface has an end-state-observable proof.
findings.finding: The 'read surface' for the claiming session id (AC: 'Read surface exposes the claiming session id for a ticket') has no specified command, API, or output format anywhere in the plan, leaving the end-state unobservable by a user or reviewer.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Read surface exposes the claiming session id for a ticket'; Success Criteria — 'queryable later'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Read surface' sub-section to Design Decisions specifying, at minimum: (1) which existing command(s) include `claimed_session` in their output (e.g. `rebar show`), (2) the output key name, and (3) a concrete example of the before/after output a user would see.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a Cursor-specific env var (e.g. `CURSOR_SESSION_ID`) to the resolver list with a design note on how it is injected, or explicitly defer/remove the Cursor shim from the AC to match the actual resolved design.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria mention 'plus Codex/Cursor shims' but no design basis for a Cursor-specific shim exists — Cursor has no env var in the resolver list and no SessionStart hook design anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims'
findings.suggested_fix: If Cursor support is in scope, add its env var to the resolver list and note its injection mechanism; if it is out of scope for this epic, remove 'Cursor' from the AC and track it as a separate child story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one user-facing validation criterion, e.g. 'Given `REBAR_SESSION_ID=abc123 rebar claim <ticket>`, running `rebar show <ticket>` displays `claiming_session: abc123`', so the improvement is demonstrable end-to-end.
findings.finding: No Acceptance Criterion or Success Criterion carries a concrete, user-facing validation mechanism (before/after workflow, operational metric, or dogfooding scenario) — all criteria remain at the internal-system level (event field written, reducer key set, shim ships).
findings.location: Success Criteria and Acceptance Criteria sections
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dogfooding/before-after AC: 'Before: `rebar show <ticket>` shows no session attribution. After: with session env var set, it shows the claiming session id.' This makes F4(d) verifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale to the Storage design decision explaining WHY inlining into STATUS `data["session"]` is the right fit (e.g., mirrors the existing `last_status_env_id` / `managed_refs` additive patterns, survives compaction by construction via compiled_state, and requires no event-type or envelope change).
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for storing session id in STATUS data["session"] (rather than a new event type or envelope field), only what it avoids — the absence of a 'why this fits' argument leaves the implementer without a design anchor.
findings.location: Design decisions / Storage section
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Storage bullet: 'This mirrors the existing additive-field pattern (e.g., last_status_env_id) — no new event type is needed, no schema version bump is required, and compaction-safety is automatic because compiled_state is verbatim-restored by process_snapshot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `remote_session` to the AC storage shape, clarify whether it follows the additive-optional pattern (present only when CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID is set, absent otherwise — like source_id), and confirm it is excluded from Jira sync and covered by the compaction-safety regression.
findings.finding: The secondary `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID` → `remote_session` field is named in the Design Decisions but is absent from the AC storage shape, make_initial_state schema, and compaction-safety discussion — making it an invisible deliverable with an unresolved data-flow.
findings.location: Design decisions / Env-var contract bullet; AC Storage shape line; reducer/_state.py make_initial_state

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the Storage design decision to explicitly say 'Record on any STATUS event that carries open→in_progress, covering both claim_core and transition_core write paths' so an implementer does not miss the transition path.
findings.finding: The `transition open→in_progress` path (distinct from `claim_core`) is mentioned in the AC but the Design Decision Storage section only says 'Record on the claim STATUS event' — an implementer reading the design may cover only claim_core and miss the parallel transition_compute path.
findings.location: Design decisions / Storage section; AC line 'Claim/transition records the id when present'; src/rebar/_commands/txn.py transition_core
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'Record on the claim STATUS event's data["session"]' to 'Record on any open→in_progress STATUS event's data["session"], applied in both claim_core (txn.py) and transition_core (txn.py).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a mechanism design for the local CLI SessionStart hook: specify the injection point (e.g., a Claude Code `PreToolCall` hook script, a shell wrapper function, or a rebar subcommand run at session start), how it generates and exports a unique per-session REBAR_SESSION_ID, and how it avoids leaking across sessions.
findings.finding: The local CLI SessionStart hook is named as a required deliverable but has no specified mechanism: what process invokes it, what event/lifecycle stage triggers it, how REBAR_SESSION_ID is scoped to one session, and whether it is a shell alias, Claude Code CLI lifecycle hook, or something else — leaving the shim unimplementable from the plan alone.
findings.location: Design decisions / Reliable capture bullet; AC 'Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims'; Success Criteria 'Works in the LOCAL Claude Code CLI'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add Cursor's native session env var to the ordered resolver (with a source citation), or explicitly describe the Cursor shim mechanism (hook name, injection point) so the AC 'Cursor shims ship' is implementable.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria explicitly include shipping a Cursor shim, but no Cursor env var appears anywhere in the ordered resolver list and no Cursor-specific shim design is described. It is unclear whether Cursor natively exports any session var (in which case an entry belongs in the resolver) or requires manual hook injection (in which case the shim design is missing).
findings.location: Design Decisions — Env-var contract (resolver list); AC bullet 7 'Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the Cursor session env var (or hook mechanism) and add it to the resolver contract, or explicitly scope the Cursor shim as a follow-on spike before committing it to this epic's AC.
findings.finding: The AC names 'Codex/Cursor shims' but no Cursor-specific env var appears in the resolver contract or anywhere in the plan, leaving it unverified whether Cursor exposes any session env var at all and under what name.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the full storage shape and reducer fold rule for the `remote_session` / `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID` secondary field (which `data{}` key, which state key, initial value in `make_initial_state`, and behavior on fork resolution).
findings.finding: The plan names a `remote_session` secondary field captured from `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID`, but never defines its storage location (which key in `data{}`, which reducer state key, whether it goes into `make_initial_state`), nor its reducer fold rule — making it an unspecified schema element that an implementer cannot derive from the plan alone.
findings.location: Design decisions > Env-var contract / Storage sections
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'remote_session' subsection to Storage: store as `data["remote_session"]` on the same STATUS event, fold to state key `claimed_remote_session` using the same last-writer-wins rule as `claimed_session`, seed `make_initial_state` with `claimed_remote_session: None`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the shared resolver function and its module location, specify its full ordered fallback chain (including disposition of the git-HEAD and 'unknown' fallbacks), and state which existing callers (transition_close, session_log, claim_core) will be migrated to use it.
findings.finding: The plan's env-var resolver contract (5-entry ordered list) is inconsistent with the two existing resolver implementations in the codebase, and the plan says 'One shared resolver replaces today's two divergent ones' but never specifies the shared resolver's name, module location, or how the existing git-HEAD and 'unknown' fallbacks are handled for the claim path.
findings.location: Design decisions > Env-var contract; src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:_resolve_session; src/rebar/_commands/session_log.py:_resolve_session_fp

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a Cursor-specific env var (e.g. `CURSOR_SESSION_ID`) to the ordered resolver contract, or explicitly document that Cursor coverage relies on `SESSION_ID` (ambient fallback) and update the AC accordingly.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria lists 'Local-CLI capture shim ships (Claude Code), plus Codex/Cursor shims' but the env-var contract contains no Cursor entry — Cursor has no env var listed — and neither `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` nor `CODEX_THREAD_ID` nor any Cursor variable appears anywhere in src/rebar/, making the Cursor shim an unspecified deliverable with no defined env var or mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria > 'Local-CLI capture shim ships'; Design decisions > Env-var contract

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the 'privy-eloquent-nauplius' alias in 6014's Scope OUT with 'c557-8f46-0c16-4bf5' or 'S5' consistently.
findings.finding: Child 6014 (S1) refers to child c557 by the internal slug alias 'privy-eloquent-nauplius' rather than by its canonical roster id 'c557-8f46-0c16-4bf5' or story label 'S5'. This alias does not appear in the complete sibling roster and cannot be resolved by tooling or reviewers consulting only the roster.
